name: n8n-claw
description: OpenClaw-inspired autonomous AI agent built entirely in n8n. Adaptive RAG-powered memory, Skills via MCP templates, proactive task management, media understanding - self-hosted with one setup script

commit.message:
fix: update CDN_BASE to latest templates commit (83a1021)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add skip options for personality/embedding prompts in setup.sh --force

When running --force on an existing installation, ask "Change personality
settings? (y/N)" and "Change embedding/RAG settings? (y/N)" before showing
the full prompt blocks. Default is No, so users can skip through quickly.

Also fixes custom persona preservation: loads existing persona from DB,
detects if it's custom (not standard template), shows it as default, and
avoids double-wrapping when kept.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: reorder README sections — Project Memory after Memory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Project Memory section to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Project Manager for persistent project memory

- New `projects` table (name, status, content as markdown, timestamps)
- New Project Manager toolCode node (list/read/create/update/archive)
- Load Active Projects node injects project names into system prompt
- Agent sees active project names on every turn, loads content on demand

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: rename MCP tools to MCP Skills in agent system prompt

- setup.sh: seed text uses "MCP Skills" terminology
- Library Manager: updateAgentConfig() generates "Skills" wording
- Agent: Build System Prompt injects "Available MCP Skills" header
- Agent: dedup regex matches both old and new section headers
- Instructs agent to use library_manager when user asks about skills

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rename MCP tools to MCP Skills, reorder sections

- Library (skill catalog) now before Builder (custom skills)
- Consistent "MCP Skills" wording throughout README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update CDN_BASE to latest templates commit (c47ece5)

Pins Library Manager to commit that includes the email-imap-smtp template.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use npm install instead of npm ci in email-bridge Dockerfile

npm ci requires package-lock.json which doesn't exist yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add email-bridge microservice for IMAP/SMTP

Stateless Node.js service exposing REST endpoints for email operations.
Runs inside Docker network only (no external port). Used by the
email-imap-smtp MCP template.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-cleanup old reminders in Memory Consolidation

Delete delivered reminders older than 30 days during the daily
consolidation run (3am). Prevents the reminders table from growing
indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deduplicate MCP server list in system prompt

Strip any static "Available MCP Servers" section from mcp_instructions
before injecting the dynamic list from mcp_registry, consistent with
how agents and history are deduplicated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: load MCP servers dynamically from mcp_registry

The agent now queries mcp_registry at runtime and injects the server
list into the system prompt. This prevents setup.sh --force from
overwriting user-added MCP servers (like NewsAPI, Wikipedia).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rename 'scheduled tasks' to 'scheduled actions' in README

Avoids confusion with the separate Task Management feature (todo list).
'Scheduled actions' clearly describes timed agent execution without
overlapping with task terminology.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update README for v0.8.0 reminders + v0.9.0 scheduled tasks

- Add scheduled tasks to feature list and architecture diagram
- Add Reminder Runner to auto-activated background workflows
- Move ReminderFactory to sub-workflows (no longer needs manual activation)
- Rewrite Reminders section: DB-backed approach, scheduled tasks, crash-safe
- Add reminders table to customization reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: escape $ in conversation content to prevent pg-promise parameter error

pg-promise interprets $N patterns as parameter placeholders. When the
agent's response contains dollar amounts (e.g. $5.000), Save Conversation
fails with "Variable $5 out of range". Fix: pgSafe() escapes $ to $$
in Prepare Save before the SQL query.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use __rl format for workflowId in Execute Agent node

n8n's Execute Workflow node requires the __rl resource locator format
for workflowId, not a plain string. Without it, n8n cannot find the
workflow to execute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add scheduled agent tasks (type='task' in reminders)

Extends the reminder system so the agent can execute tasks at scheduled
times, not just send text messages. When type='task', the ReminderRunner
triggers the agent workflow via Execute Workflow instead of sending a
Telegram message directly.

Changes:
- Schema: add type column to reminders table (with idempotent migration)
- ReminderFactory: parse and store type parameter
- ReminderRunner: Switch node routes reminder vs task, Execute Workflow
  node triggers agent for tasks
- Agent: second trigger (executeWorkflowTrigger), Merge Input node to
  unify Telegram and scheduled task paths, scheduled task hint in system
  prompt
- setup.sh: patch Agent workflow ID in ReminderRunner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: default user_id to telegram chat ID instead of 'unknown'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use number type for If node in Reminder Runner

The id field from PostgREST is a number, not a string. Strict type
validation with string/exists crashes. Use number/exists with loose
validation instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use /rest/v1/ prefix and apikey header for PostgREST

Kong routes only /rest/v1/* to PostgREST. Without the prefix,
requests get 404. Also use apikey header instead of Authorization
Bearer (Kong key-auth plugin requirement).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: replace per-reminder workflows with single polling runner (#8)

Instead of creating a new n8n workflow for every reminder (fragile,
clutters workflow list), reminders are now stored in a `reminders`
DB table. A single Reminder Runner workflow polls every minute and
sends due reminders via Telegram.

- Add `reminders` table with `reminded_at` flag for delivery tracking
- Simplify ReminderFactory to a single DB insert (no more API calls)
- Add Reminder Runner workflow (schedule trigger → poll → send → mark)
- Missed reminders are delivered on next poll (crash-safe)

Closes #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deduplicate system prompt to reduce token usage by ~30x

Sequential Postgres node chaining caused cartesian product: 6 soul rows
× 6 agents rows = 36x duplication of agents config and conversation
history. System prompt was 156K chars (~39K tokens) instead of ~5K chars
(~1.2K tokens). Deduplicate agents by key and conversation history by
content in Build System Prompt code node.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Credential Flow to README

Document secure one-time links for API keys, credential-form workflow,
and template_credentials/credential_tokens DB tables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use helpers instead of $helpers in credential-form Code node

$helpers is undefined in Code node v2 — must use helpers.httpRequest().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use webhookId-based form URL for credential links

The credential form is registered at /form/{webhookId}, not /form/credential-form
(n8n webhook registration bug). Library Manager now uses a CREDENTIAL_FORM_PATH
constant with {{CREDENTIAL_FORM_WEBHOOK_ID}} placeholder, replaced by setup.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pin CDN URL to commit hash for instant cache invalidation

jsDelivr caches @master for hours. Pinning to commit hash ensures
template changes are immediately available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace Supabase placeholders in template workflows during install

Templates fetched from CDN contain {{SUPABASE_URL}} and
{{SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY}} placeholders in Code node JS. Without this
fix, these remain as literal strings and DB queries in installed
templates fail silently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: webhookId for form trigger, cred_key optional, activate credential-form

- Add webhookId UUID to credential-form.json so custom path registers
  correctly after API import (n8n webhook registration bug fix)
- Make cred_key optional in add_credential action — without it,
  generates links for ALL required credentials from manifest
- Activate credential-form workflow in setup.sh
- Update agent tool description for simplified UX

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rewrite credential-form without Respond to Webhook nodes

n8n Form Trigger does not support Respond to Webhook nodes.
Simplified to: Form Trigger → single Code Node (validate + save).
Errors throw, success shows form completion page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set N8N_URL to public domain in setup.sh

N8N_URL was never updated from localhost:5678 when a domain was
configured, causing credential form links to point to localhost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove pinData and webhookId from credential-form workflow

n8n API rejects unknown top-level properties like pinData.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Credential Flow (Phase 2) — secure one-time links for API keys

- Add credential_tokens + template_credentials tables to DB schema
- New credential-form workflow: Form Trigger → validate token → save credential
- Library Manager: add_credential action + auto-generate links after install
- Agent tool description updated with add_credential
- setup.sh: credential-form added to IMPORT_ORDER

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove local docs from repo, gitignore docs/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #7 from freddy-schuetz/fix/issues-4-5-docs

fix: OpenAI node v2.1 + timezone bug + docs update

commit.message:
docs: update template-registry and implementierung for v0.6.0

- Mark Phase 1 tasks as completed
- Update template list, manifest format, CDN strategy
- Add Phase 2 Credential Flow plan details

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update OpenAI node to v2.1 and fix timezone in Task Manager

- OpenAI "Transcribe Voice" node: typeVersion 1.8 → 2.1 (fixes #4)
- Task Manager summary: use user timezone from user_profiles instead of
  UTC for overdue/due-today calculation (fixes #5)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #3 from lcx/feature/skip-nginx

add feature to allow skipping of nginx + LE

add feature to allow skipping of nginx + LE

commit.message:
add feature to allow skipping of nginx + LE

If you already have a reverse proxy in front of this which takes care of
SSL, activate this option to prevent configuring a new ngingx + Let's
encrypt

docs: add Library Manager section to README, translate workflow messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: translate Library Manager messages to English

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: log Library Manager ID in wiring output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: retry agent activation on rate limit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add MCP Template Registry & Library Manager

- Add Library Manager workflow (list/install/remove templates from catalog)
- Add Library Manager as agent tool in n8n-claw-agent workflow
- Extend mcp_registry schema (template_id, template_type, sub_workflow_id)
- Update setup.sh import order and ID patching
- Add n8n-claw-templates/ to .gitignore (separate repo)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: improve MCP Builder search result selection

- Fix undefined API name in search query by parsing task from JSON input
- Add Pick Best Result node between Search API Docs and Fetch Doc Content
- Score results by keyword relevance, domain matching, and API doc patterns
- Penalize generic sites (stackoverflow, reddit, etc.)
- Use engines=google,duckduckgo,brave instead of categories=it

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add welcome message on first contact (onboarding)

Agent introduces itself and lists capabilities with examples on
first Telegram message. Uses setup_done flag from user_profiles.
After first response, Prepare Save node automatically sets
setup_done = true via SQL — no LLM tool call needed.

Also: migrate MCP Builder search from Brave API to self-hosted
SearXNG (no API key needed, better results via aggregation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Supabase Studio to stack section in README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add SearXNG to stack section in README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add web search via self-hosted SearXNG

Adds a SearXNG container to the Docker stack for API-key-free web search.
The AI agent can now search the web using a new Web Search tool.

- SearXNG container in docker-compose.yml (port 8888, internal 8080)
- searxng/settings.yml with JSON output enabled and multi-engine search
- Web Search toolCode node connected to AI Agent
- Tool hint in system prompt to guide agent usage
- setup.sh generates SearXNG secret key automatically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add Nominatim reverse geocoding hint to location messages

Ensures the AI agent reliably resolves coordinates to addresses
by providing the Nominatim API URL directly in the location message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update README media support to reflect actual implementation

Photos use OpenAI Vision (GPT-4o-mini), not Claude Vision.
PDFs use built-in text extraction, not Claude multimodal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add OpenAI Vision for photo analysis, fix response parsing

Photos are now analyzed by GPT-4o-mini Vision instead of relying on
passthroughBinaryImages (which doesn't work reliably). The image
description is passed as text to the AI Agent.

- Add Analyze Photo node (OpenAI, resource=image, operation=analyze)
- Route: Get Photo File → Analyze Photo → Normalize Message
- Fix Normalize Message to parse OpenAI array response format
  (json[0].content[0].text)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: photo crash and PDF hallucination in media handling

- Switch node: set looseTypeValidation=true (strict mode crashed on
  arrays and missing fields)
- Photo condition: type "object" → "array" (message.photo is an array
  of photo sizes)
- PDF: add Extract PDF Text node (extractFromFile, operation=pdf) to
  extract text content instead of passing binary through
  (passthroughBinaryImages only works for images, not PDFs)
- Normalize Message: read extracted PDF text instead of binary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reuse OpenAI embedding key for voice transcription

Instead of asking for the OpenAI API key twice (once for embeddings,
once for Whisper), the key is now automatically reused when the
embedding provider is OpenAI. A separate prompt only appears if the
user chose a different provider (Voyage/Ollama) or skipped embeddings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add media handling for voice, photos, documents, and location

Telegram messages with voice notes, photos, PDFs, and shared locations
are now processed instead of silently ignored. Voice is transcribed via
OpenAI Whisper, images and PDFs pass through to Claude Vision natively,
and locations are converted to coordinate text.

- Add 8 new nodes: Switch (Route Media Type), Telegram getFile x3,
  OpenAI Whisper transcription, Format Location, Unsupported Media,
  Normalize Message
- Update Build System Prompt to read from Normalize Message
- Add optional OpenAI API Key prompt and credential to setup.sh
- Add Media Support section to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: setup.sh bugfixes from code review

- Restore set -e after credential block (errors were silently swallowed)
- Normalize PROACTIVE_CHOICE default so heartbeat is enabled when user
  presses Enter (choosing the default proactive behavior)
- Add CYAN color variable (was used but never defined)
- Clean up /tmp/pg-cred.json after credential creation
- Add failure exit to first DB wait loop (matching second loop pattern)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: instruct agent to create both reminder AND task for "remind me" requests

Previously, "remind me to X" only triggered the Reminder tool (timed
Telegram notification) but not the Task Manager. The task was never
saved, so it didn't appear in task lists or morning briefings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: remove German examples from README, fix duplicate update section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: comprehensive README overhaul for completeness and clarity

- Rewritten architecture diagram: separate agent tools from background
  workflows (Heartbeat, Memory Consolidation)
- New section: Memory (automatic + manual saving, semantic search,
  consolidation explained with examples)
- New section: Reminders (with chat examples)
- Workflow table split into auto-activated / manual / sub-workflows
- Expanded Customization table: added memory_daily, tools_config
- New troubleshooting entries: heartbeat, memory search
- Clearer update vs --force distinction
- Added intro sentence to "What it does"
- Split heartbeat/morning briefing into separate bullet points

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Task Management, Heartbeat & Morning Briefing to README

Document new features with usage examples: creating/checking/updating
tasks via chat, enabling proactive heartbeat, setting up morning
briefing. Updated architecture diagram and workflow table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Update Heartbeat reads reason from Call Claude Haiku, not Telegram

$input.first() reads from Telegram Send which returns the Telegram API
response (no reason field). This caused morning_briefing last_run to
never be updated, breaking the duplicate-send prevention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: heartbeat uses user language + rate-limit morning briefing

- Load user's preferred language from user_profiles.preferences
  and pass to Haiku prompt ("Respond in German/English/...")
- Add 1h minimum interval for morning briefing to prevent
  duplicate sends on rapid manual triggers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle empty response body in Task Manager pgrest helper

PostgREST returns empty body for PATCH with Prefer: return=minimal.
JSON.parse('') threw an error, causing set_preference to fail on
the heartbeat_config update even though user_profiles was saved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: explicitly disable heartbeat when user chooses reactive mode

ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING preserves existing enabled=true, so reactive
choice must explicitly SET enabled=false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enable heartbeat automatically when user chooses proactive behavior

If user selects proactive mode during setup personalization, heartbeat
is enabled in heartbeat_config. Reactive mode keeps it disabled.
Only runs when personalization was actually shown (fresh install or --force).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rename LANG to PREFERRED_LANG to avoid Linux env var collision

LANG=C is used by psql for locale. Using LANG for preferred language
collided with this. Renamed to PREFERRED_LANG and now persists in
user_profiles.preferences.language (preserved across --force runs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: query user_profiles by telegram chat ID + remove placeholder seed

- EXISTING_USER/TZ/CTX queries now filter by telegram:CHAT_ID
  (previously LIMIT 1 could return placeholder row instead of real data)
- Removed placeholder user_profiles row from 002_seed.sql
  (setup.sh creates the real profile during personalization)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: persist user profile to DB + skip duplicate credentials on --force

- Write display_name, timezone, context to user_profiles after personalization
  (so --force picks up existing values as defaults next time)
- Check for existing Telegram/Postgres credentials before creating new ones
  (prevents duplicate credentials on repeated --force runs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: escape curly braces in task_management f-string (ValueError)

The JSON example in the task_management agents seed used literal
{} which Python's f-string interpreted as format specifiers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Heartbeat + Task Management (Phase 2+3)

- New tasks table in schema (status, priority, due_date, parent_id for subtasks)
- Task Manager toolCode node on agent (list/create/update/delete/summary/set_preference)
- Heartbeat workflow: schedule trigger every 15min, checks tasks + user prefs,
  Claude Haiku decides whether to send proactive Telegram message
- Morning briefing support via user_profiles.preferences
- Heartbeat starts disabled by default — user enables via agent conversation
- setup.sh: heartbeat in IMPORT_ORDER, heartbeat_config seeds, activation,
  task_management agent instructions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: mention memory_behavior in agents table description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: detailed memory behavior instructions for agent

Agent now has explicit rules for when to save and search memory:
- Save: preferences, personal facts, decisions, explicit requests
- Search: before recommendations, when topics recur, when asked
- New 'memory_behavior' key: active recall, learn from corrections

These instructions are seeded via setup.sh for all new installs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use this.helpers.httpRequest in consolidation + ask Anthropic key in setup

- Memory Consolidation Code node used $helpers (undefined in Code nodes).
  Correct syntax for n8n-nodes-base.code is this.helpers.httpRequest.
- setup.sh now asks for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY during setup, writes it to
  tools_config so the consolidation workflow can call Claude Haiku.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: create roles before GRANTs in schema (fresh install fix)

On fresh installs, all GRANTs for anon/service_role/authenticated
failed silently because the roles were created 900 lines later.
Move role creation to top of schema, right after extensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use explicit schema for CREATE EXTENSION (empty search_path)

pg_dump clears the search_path, causing CREATE EXTENSION to fail with
"no schema has been selected to create in" on fresh installs. Adding
SCHEMA public fixes this for both uuid-ossp and vector extensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: RAG pipeline — vector memory with consolidation

Adds vector-based memory search (pgvector + HNSW), memory consolidation
workflow, DB-based API key config (tools_config table), and fixes for
setup.sh idempotency, Kong auth, and schema robustness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: mount kong.deployed.yml instead of template with placeholders

docker-compose.yml was mounting supabase/kong.yml (template with
{{SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY}} placeholders) instead of kong.deployed.yml
(generated by setup.sh with actual JWT keys). This caused Kong to
reject ALL PostgREST requests with 401, breaking Memory Save/Search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add pgvector extension, authenticated role, and HNSW index to schema

001_schema.sql was missing CREATE EXTENSION vector, causing memory_long
table creation to silently fail. Also adds authenticated role (referenced
by 27 GRANTs but never created) and switches IVFFlat to HNSW index
(works on empty tables). setup.sh now shows schema errors instead of
hiding them and reloads PostgREST cache after schema apply.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: move API keys from env vars to DB (tools_config table)

Replace $env access (blocked by default in n8n) with PostgREST reads
from the tools_config table. setup.sh writes embedding + anthropic
config to DB during setup. Workflows read config at runtime via
PostgREST — no container restart needed when config changes.

Removes EMBEDDING_* and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from docker-compose.yml
environment since they are no longer read as env vars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: preserve existing config as defaults in --force mode

Load current personalization from DB (soul + user_profiles) and show as
defaults in cli_ask prompts. Users can press Enter to keep values or type
new ones. Also show embedding key status when already configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent duplicate workflows on setup.sh --force

Replace POST-only workflow import with upsert logic: check if a workflow
with the same name exists before importing. If yes, update via PUT
(preserving the workflow ID). If no, create via POST as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: RAG pipeline — vector memory search with configurable embeddings

- Add Memory Consolidation workflow (daily scheduled, Claude Haiku summary + embedding)
- Update Memory Save tool to generate embeddings before storing
- Update Memory Search tool with vector search + keyword fallback
- Add configurable embedding provider (openai/voyage/ollama) via env vars
- Add EMBEDDING_* and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env vars to docker-compose.yml
- Update setup.sh: .env auto-merge for new keys, selective workflow import
  in update mode, embedding prompt for both fresh and update installs
- Update README with RAG documentation and architecture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: fix inaccuracies in README

- Add Anthropic API Key to setup config list
- Note Telegram Bot credential is auto-created
- Clarify which workflows need manual activation
- Fix Studio URL (localhost only, not public IP)
- Fix Step reference in troubleshooting (Step 2, not 3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto git pull in update mode

setup.sh now runs git pull --ff-only automatically when updating.
Falls back gracefully if there are local changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add update mode, timezone, and troubleshooting to README

- Document setup.sh update mode and --force flag
- Add timezone to setup wizard step list
- Add wrong time troubleshooting entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-detect timezone in setup wizard

Detects system timezone via timedatectl/etc/timezone and shows it as
default during setup. User can override interactively. Fallback is UTC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use TIMEZONE env var for user profile instead of hardcoded UTC

setup.sh now uses the TIMEZONE variable (default: Europe/Berlin) when
seeding user_profiles, so the agent shows the correct local time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: inject current date/time into agent system prompt

Uses n8n's $now (Luxon) with user's timezone from profile (UTC fallback).
The agent can now answer time-related questions correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: create supabase_admin role before uuid-ossp extension

The Supabase postgres image has extension control files that reference
supabase_admin as owner. CREATE EXTENSION fails without this role.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: uuid-ossp extension for fresh installs (3-layer fix)

n8n's PostgreSQL migrations need uuid_generate_v4() but the extension
was never created before n8n started. Three fixes:

1. New 000_extensions.sql — runs before 001_schema.sql in
   docker-entrypoint-initdb.d (alphabetical order)
2. CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS in 001_schema.sql — prevents
   ON_ERROR_STOP from aborting the whole script
3. setup.sh starts DB first, creates extension via psql,
   THEN starts n8n — guarantees extension exists before migrations

Also adds docs/feature-roadmap.md to .gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fresh install crash — uuid extension + .env trailing spaces

Two bugs preventing fresh installs on new VPS:

1. n8n's PostgreSQL migration needs uuid_generate_v4() but the
   uuid-ossp extension was never created. Added CREATE EXTENSION
   to 001_schema.sql. (Was not needed before because n8n used
   SQLite until the postgres backend switch in 9884c72.)

2. .env.example had inline comments (KEY=value  # comment) causing
   trailing whitespace in values. N8N_PROTOCOL got 'http           '
   instead of 'http'. Fixed by moving comments to separate lines
   and stripping trailing whitespace in _load_env.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: declare WF_IDS before install-mode guard to prevent syntax error

declare -A WF_IDS was inside the workflow import block which gets
skipped in update mode, but WF_IDS was referenced outside the guard
at line 492 → bash syntax error on associative array access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: pull latest Docker images in update mode

Without docker compose pull, the update mode reuses cached images
and never actually updates n8n or other services.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: _load_env regex now handles digits in variable names (N8N_*)

The regex ^[A-Z_]+ did not match variable names containing digits,
so all N8N_* variables were silently never exported from .env.
This caused setup.sh to re-prompt for the API key on every update run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use python3 JSON parsing for volume encryption key recovery

The grep pattern did not match because the JSON config has a space
after the colon ("encryptionKey": "...") but grep expected no space.
This caused volume key recovery to silently fail on EVERY update run,
generating a new key each time → encryption key mismatch → n8n crash.

Now uses python3 json.load() for robust parsing. Also adds:
- Warning when .env key differs from volume key
- Error message when volume key cannot be read

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct volume config path for encryption key recovery

The n8n config file is at /data/config, not /data/.n8n/config.
Now checks both paths for compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add update mode to setup.sh (safe re-run on existing installs)

- Detect existing installation via Docker volume presence
- Recover encryption key from n8n volume (prevents mismatch crash)
- Skip credential creation and workflow import in update mode
- Add --force flag to reimport workflows and reconfigure personality
- Introduce set_env() helper to prevent .env key duplication
- Remove duplicate key generation block (was redundant)
- Add N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY to .env.example with DO NOT CHANGE warning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add private dev context system (CLAUDE.local.md)

- .gitignore: add CLAUDE.local.md so private dev context can never be committed
- CLAUDE.md: add "Local Dev Context" section explaining the two-file pattern
- CLAUDE.local.example.md: public template for CLAUDE.local.md

Private dev data (instance URL, SSH, workflow IDs) goes in gitignored
CLAUDE.local.md or docs/infrastructure.md — never in public CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: ignore docs/infrastructure.md

Instance-specific operational docs must not be committed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: add local dev files to .gitignore

scripts/, .mcp.json and .claude/ are local development helpers
and must not be committed to the public repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: v0.10.1 — Setup UX improvements
releases.body:
## What's new

- **Setup skip options**: When running `./setup.sh --force` on an existing installation, personality and embedding prompts can now be skipped with a simple y/N question (default: No)
- **Custom persona preservation**: Existing custom personas are loaded from the database and preserved during `--force` updates instead of being overwritten

## Upgrade

```bash
cd /root/n8n-claw && git pull && ./setup.sh --force
```

releases.name: v0.10.0 — Project Memory
releases.body:
## What's new

### Project Memory
Persistent markdown documents for tracking ongoing work across conversations — like a living notebook per topic.

- **Create** projects via natural language: "I'm working on a presentation about AI"
- **Read** project details: "What's the status of the presentation?"
- **Update** incrementally: "Add to the presentation: slide 3 should show statistics"
- **List** all projects: "What projects do I have?"
- **Archive** completed work: "The presentation is done"

Active project names are injected into the agent's system prompt on every turn — the agent always knows what you're working on, but loads full content only on demand (token-efficient).

### Files changed
- `supabase/migrations/001_schema.sql` — new `projects` table
- `workflows/n8n-claw-agent.json` — Project Manager tool + Load Active Projects node + system prompt injection
- `README.md` — new Project Memory section

releases.name: v0.9.3 — Email Bridge + MCP Skills
releases.body:
## What's new

### Email Bridge
- New `email-bridge` microservice (Node.js/Express) for IMAP/SMTP operations via REST
- Stateless design — credentials passed per request, nothing stored in the bridge
- Runs inside Docker network only (no external port exposure)
- Supports the new `email-imap-smtp` template from n8n-claw-templates

### MCP Skills Rename
- "MCP tools/templates" renamed to "MCP Skills" throughout the project
- Agent system prompt updated — now uses `library_manager` with `list_templates` when users ask about available skills
- README reordered: Skills Library before Building custom Skills (catalog-first approach)

### Files changed
- `email-bridge/` — new microservice (server.js, Dockerfile, package.json)
- `docker-compose.yml` — added email-bridge service
- `workflows/n8n-claw-agent.json` — MCP Skills wording + dedup regex
- `workflows/mcp-library-manager.json` — MCP Skills wording + CDN hash update
- `setup.sh` — MCP Skills wording in agent seed data
- `README.md` — reordered sections, MCP Skills terminology

releases.name: v0.9.2 — Auto-cleanup old reminders
releases.body:
## What's new

### Auto-cleanup delivered reminders
The Memory Consolidation workflow (runs daily at 3am) now automatically deletes reminders that were delivered more than 30 days ago. This prevents the `reminders` table from growing indefinitely.

## Upgrade

```bash
./setup.sh --force
```

releases.name: v0.9.1 — Dynamic MCP server loading
releases.body:
## What's new

### Dynamic MCP server loading
The agent now queries `mcp_registry` at runtime and injects the server list into the system prompt dynamically. Previously, the server list was hardcoded in the `agents.mcp_instructions` DB row and got overwritten on every `setup.sh --force` update — wiping user-added MCP servers (like NewsAPI, Wikipedia).

### Changes
- **New node "Load MCP Servers"** in the agent workflow — queries `mcp_registry WHERE active = true`
- **Build System Prompt** strips any static server section from `mcp_instructions` and replaces it with the dynamic list (consistent deduplication pattern)
- **setup.sh** no longer hardcodes specific servers in `mcp_instructions` — the dynamic loader handles it

### Also included (since v0.6.0)
- **v0.7.0**: Credential Flow — secure one-time links for API keys
- **v0.8.0**: DB-backed reminders with single polling Reminder Runner
- **v0.9.0**: Scheduled actions — agent executes instructions at a set time
- **Fixes**: pgSafe `$` escaping, `__rl` format for Execute Workflow node, system prompt deduplication

## Upgrade

```bash
./setup.sh --force
```

Existing MCP servers in `mcp_registry` are preserved. The agent will automatically pick them up on next message.

readme_html:
🤖 n8n-claw — Self-Hosted AI Agent

A fully self-hosted AI agent built on n8n + PostgreSQL + Claude. Talks to you via Telegram, builds its own MCP tools, manages reminders and memory — all running on your own infrastructure.

What it does

Talk to your agent in natural language — it manages tasks, remembers context across conversations, builds API integrations, and proactively keeps you on track.

  • Telegram chat — talk to your AI agent directly via Telegram
  • Long-term memory — remembers conversations and important context with optional semantic search (RAG)
  • Task management — create, track, and complete tasks with priorities and due dates
  • Proactive heartbeat — automatically reminds you of overdue/urgent tasks
  • Morning briefing — daily summary of your tasks at a time you choose
  • MCP Skills — install pre-built skills or build new API integrations on demand
  • Smart reminders — timed Telegram reminders ("remind me in 2 hours to...")
  • Scheduled actions — the agent executes instructions at a set time ("search HN for AI news at 9am")
  • Web search — searches the web via built-in SearXNG instance (no API key needed)
  • Project memory — persistent markdown documents for tracking ongoing work across conversations
  • Extensible — add new skills and capabilities through natural language or from the skill catalog

Architecture

Telegram
  ↓
n8n-claw Agent (Claude Sonnet)
  ├── Task Manager        — create, track, complete tasks
  ├── Project Manager     — persistent project notes (markdown)
  ├── Memory Save/Search  — long-term memory with vector search
  ├── MCP Client          → calls tools on MCP skill servers
  ├── Library Manager     → install/remove skills from catalog
  ├── MCP Builder          → builds custom skills from scratch
  ├── Reminder            — timed reminders + scheduled actions
  ├── HTTP Tool           — simple web requests
  ├── Web Search          — search the web (SearXNG)
  └── Self Modify         — inspect/list n8n workflows

Background Workflows (automated):
  💓 Heartbeat              — every 15 min: proactive reminders + morning briefing
  🧠 Memory Consolidation   — daily at 3am: summarizes conversations → long-term memory
  ⏰ Reminder Runner         — every 1 min: sends due reminders + triggers scheduled actions

Installation

What you need

  • A Linux VPS (Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 recommended, also tested with Debian 13, 2GB RAM and 12GB Disk minimum)
  • A Telegram Bot — create one via @BotFather
  • Your Telegram Chat ID — get it from @userinfobot
  • An Anthropic API Key — from console.anthropic.com
  • A domain name (optional but recommended, required for Telegram HTTPS webhooks)

Step 1 — Clone & run

git clone https://github.com/freddy-schuetz/n8n-claw.git && cd n8n-claw && ./setup.sh

The script will:

  1. Update the system (apt update && apt upgrade)
  2. Install Docker automatically if not present
  3. Start n8n so you can generate an API key
  4. Ask you for configuration interactively:
    • n8n API Key (generated in n8n UI → Settings → API)
    • Telegram Bot Token
    • Telegram Chat ID
    • Anthropic API Key
    • Domain name (optional — enables HTTPS via Let's Encrypt + nginx, or skip nginx if you already have a reverse proxy)
  5. Configure your agent's personality:
    • Agent name
    • Your name
    • Preferred language
    • Timezone (auto-detected from system)
    • Communication style (casual / professional / friendly)
    • Proactive vs reactive behavior
    • Free-text custom persona (overrides the above)
  6. Start all services (n8n, PostgreSQL, PostgREST, Kong)
  7. Apply database schema and seed data
  8. Create n8n credentials (Telegram Bot automatically)
  9. Import all workflows into n8n
  10. Wire workflow references (MCP Builder, Reminders, etc.)
  11. Activate the agent automatically

Step 2 — Add credentials in n8n UI

Open n8n at the URL shown at the end of setup.

The easiest way is to open each workflow and click "Create new credential" directly on the node that needs it. n8n will prompt you automatically.

Credentials you'll need:

Credential Name (exact!) Where needed
Postgres Supabase Postgres Agent (Load Soul, Load History, etc.)
Anthropic API Anthropic API Agent (Claude node), MCP Builder
Telegram Bot Telegram Bot Agent (Telegram Trigger + Reply) — created automatically by setup
OpenAI API OpenAI API Agent (Voice transcription via Whisper) — optional, created by setup if key provided

Postgres connection details (shown in setup output):

  • Host: db | Port: 5432 | DB: postgres | User: postgres
  • Password: (shown at end of setup)
  • SSL: disable

MCP Builder — select LLM model:

  • Open the MCP Builder workflow → click the LLM node
  • Select Anthropic API as the chat model
  • (not set automatically due to n8n credential linking)

Optional: Embeddings for semantic memory search:

During setup, you'll be asked for an embedding API key. This enables vector-based memory search (RAG) — the agent can find memories by meaning, not just exact keywords.

  • OpenAI (default): text-embedding-3-small — platform.openai.com (requires API key)
  • Voyage AI: voyage-3-lite — voyageai.com (free tier available)
  • Ollama: nomic-embed-text — local, no API key needed (requires Ollama running on your server)

Without an embedding key, the agent still works — it falls back to keyword-based memory search.

Optional: OpenAI API Key for voice messages:

If you chose OpenAI as your embedding provider, the same key is automatically used for voice transcription (Whisper) — no extra input needed. If you use a different embedding provider (or none), setup will ask separately for an OpenAI key. Without it, voice messages won't work — but photos, documents, and locations work without any extra keys.

Step 3 — Activate remaining workflows

These workflows are activated automatically by setup — no action needed:

Workflow Purpose
🤖 n8n-claw Agent Main agent — receives Telegram messages, calls tools
💓 Heartbeat Background: proactive reminders + morning briefing (every 15 min)
🧠 Memory Consolidation Background: summarizes conversations into long-term memory (daily 3am)
⏰ Reminder Runner Background: delivers reminders + triggers scheduled actions (every 1 min)

These workflows need to be activated manually in n8n UI:

Workflow Purpose
🏗️ MCP Builder Builds custom MCP skills on demand
🌤️ MCP: Weather Example MCP Server — weather via Open-Meteo (no API key)
⚙️ WorkflowBuilder Builds general n8n automations (optional — requires extra setup)

Sub-workflows (called by other workflows, no manual activation needed):

Workflow Called by
🔌 MCP Client Agent — calls tools on MCP skill servers
📚 MCP Library Manager Agent — installs/removes skills from catalog
⏰ ReminderFactory Agent — saves reminders/tasks to database
🔐 credential-form Library Manager — secure form for entering API keys

Step 4 — Start chatting

Send a message to your Telegram bot. It's ready!

Services & URLs

After setup, these services run:

Service URL Purpose
n8n http://YOUR-IP:5678 Workflow editor
Supabase Studio http://localhost:3001 (via SSH tunnel) Database admin UI
PostgREST API http://YOUR-IP:8000 REST API for PostgreSQL

Accessing Supabase Studio

Supabase Studio is bound to localhost only (not publicly exposed). To access it from your browser, open an SSH tunnel:

ssh -L 3001:localhost:3001 user@YOUR-VPS-IP

Then open http://localhost:3001 in your browser. The tunnel stays open as long as the SSH session runs.

MCP Skills Library

Install pre-built skills from the skill catalog — no coding required. Just ask your agent:

"What skills are available?" "Install weather-openmeteo" "Remove weather-openmeteo"

The Library Manager fetches skill templates from GitHub, imports the workflows into n8n, and registers the new MCP server automatically.

⚠️ After installing a skill: deactivate → reactivate the new MCP workflow in n8n UI (required due to a webhook registration bug in n8n).

Skills with API keys: Some skills require an API key (e.g. NewsAPI). When you install one, the agent sends you a secure one-time link via Telegram. Click it, enter your key — done. The key is stored in the database and the skill reads it at runtime. Links expire after 10 minutes and can only be used once.

"Install news-newsapi" → Agent sends a link to enter your NewsAPI key → Enter key in the form → skill works immediately

You can also regenerate a credential link later:

"Add credential for news-newsapi"

Want to create your own skills? See the template contribution guide.

Building custom MCP Skills

For APIs not covered by the skill catalog, ask your agent to build one from scratch:

"Build me an MCP server for the OpenLibrary API — look up books by ISBN"

The MCP Builder will:

  1. Search for API documentation automatically (via SearXNG + Jina Reader)
  2. Generate working tool code
  3. Deploy two new n8n workflows (MCP trigger + sub-workflow)
  4. Register the server in the database
  5. Update the agent so it knows about the new tool

⚠️ After each MCP build: deactivate → reactivate the new MCP workflow in n8n UI (same webhook bug as skill install).

Memory

The agent has a multi-layered memory system — it remembers things you tell it and learns from your conversations over time.

Automatic memory: The agent decides on its own what's worth remembering from your conversations (preferences, facts about you, decisions). No action needed.

Manual memory: You can also explicitly ask it to remember something:

"Remember that I prefer morning meetings before 10am" "Remember that I take my coffee black"

Memory search: When relevant, the agent searches its memory to give you contextual answers. With an embedding API key (configured during setup), it uses semantic search — finding memories by meaning, not just keywords.

"What do you know about my coffee preferences?" "What did we discuss about the server migration?"

Memory Consolidation runs automatically every night at 3am. It summarizes the day's conversations into concise long-term memories with vector embeddings. This keeps the memory efficient and searchable. Requires an embedding API key (OpenAI, Voyage AI, or Ollama — configured during setup).

Project Memory

Track ongoing work across multiple conversations with persistent project documents. Each project is a markdown file the agent creates, reads, and updates on demand — like a living notebook for each topic you're working on.

Creating a project:

"I'm working on a presentation about AI in tourism" "New project: server migration to Hetzner"

The agent creates a structured markdown document with goals, notes, and open items.

Checking projects:

"What projects do I have?" "What's the status of the presentation?"

Active project names are shown to the agent automatically — it always knows what you're working on.

Updating a project:

"Add to the presentation: slide 3 should show statistics" "Update the server migration: DNS is now configured"

The agent reads the current document, adds your notes, and saves the updated version.

Archiving:

"The presentation is done"

Sets the project status to completed — it disappears from the active list but stays in the database.

Task Management

The agent can manage tasks for you — just tell it what you need in natural language.

Creating tasks:

"Remind me to call the dentist tomorrow" "Create a task: prepare presentation for Friday, high priority" "I need to buy groceries by Saturday"

Checking tasks:

"What are my tasks?" "Show me overdue tasks" "Task summary"

Updating tasks:

"Mark the dentist task as done" "Cancel the groceries task" "Change the presentation priority to urgent"

Tasks support priorities (low, medium, high, urgent), due dates, and subtasks.

Reminders & Scheduled Actions

The agent supports two types of timed actions:

Reminders — sends a Telegram message at the specified time:

"Remind me in 30 minutes to check the oven" "Remind me tomorrow at 9am about the doctor's appointment" "Set a reminder for Friday at 3pm: submit the report"

Scheduled Actions — the agent actively executes instructions at the specified time and sends the result:

"Search Hacker News for AI articles at 9am and list them" "Check the weather forecast for Berlin tomorrow at 7am and send me a summary"

Both are stored in the database and delivered by the Reminder Runner — a background workflow that polls every minute. Missed reminders (e.g. if n8n was briefly down) are automatically delivered on the next run.

Media Support

The agent understands more than just text — send voice messages, photos, documents, or locations directly in Telegram.

Media type What happens Requires
Voice messages Transcribed via OpenAI Whisper, then processed as text OpenAI API Key
Photos Analyzed via OpenAI Vision (GPT-4o-mini), description passed to agent OpenAI API Key
Documents (PDF) Text extracted via n8n's built-in PDF parser, passed to agent — (built-in)
Location Converted to coordinates text, agent responds with context — (built-in)

Voice and photo analysis require an OpenAI API key (configured during setup). Without it, voice messages and photos won't work — but documents and locations function without any extra API keys.

[send a voice message] — automatically transcribed and answered [send a photo] — "What do you see?" — analyzed by GPT-4o-mini Vision [send a PDF] — text extracted and analyzed by the agent [share location] — agent responds with location context

Heartbeat & Morning Briefing

The Heartbeat is a background workflow that runs every 15 minutes. It checks for overdue or urgent tasks and sends you a short Telegram reminder — without you having to ask.

Proactive reminders are enabled automatically if you chose "Proactive" during setup. You can also toggle them via chat:

"Enable the heartbeat" / "Disable proactive messages"

Rate-limited to one message every 2 hours (configurable) — no spam.

Morning Briefing sends you a daily summary at your chosen time:

"Enable morning briefing at 8am" "Set morning briefing to 7:30" "Disable morning briefing"

The briefing includes: overdue tasks, today's tasks, and a short motivating note — in your preferred language.

Customization

Edit the soul and agents tables directly in Supabase Studio (http://localhost:3001 via SSH tunnel) to change your agent's personality, tools, and behavior — no code changes needed.

Table Contents
soul Agent personality (name, persona, vibe, boundaries) — loaded into system prompt
agents Tool instructions, MCP config, memory behavior — loaded into system prompt
user_profiles User name, timezone, preferences (language, morning briefing)
tasks Task management (title, status, priority, due date, subtasks)
projects Project documents (name, status, markdown content)
reminders Scheduled reminders + tasks (message, time, type, delivery status)
heartbeat_config Heartbeat + morning briefing settings (enabled, last_run, intervals)
tools_config API keys for Anthropic, embedding provider — used by Heartbeat + Consolidation
mcp_registry Available MCP servers (name, URL, tools)
template_credentials API keys for MCP templates (entered via credential form)
credential_tokens One-time tokens for secure credential entry (10 min TTL)
conversations Full chat history (session-based)
memory_long Long-term memory with vector embeddings (semantic search)
memory_daily Daily interaction log (used by Memory Consolidation)

HTTPS Setup

If you provided a domain during setup, HTTPS is configured automatically via Let's Encrypt + nginx. This is the default and works for most people. If not, you can add it later:

DOMAIN=n8n.yourdomain.com ./setup.sh

Point your domain's DNS A record to the VPS IP before running this.

Already have a reverse proxy?

If you're running your own reverse proxy (Caddy, Traefik, nginx on another host, etc.), setup will ask whether to skip the built-in nginx + Let's Encrypt installation. Answer yes to skip — n8n will still be configured with the correct HTTPS webhook URLs, but TLS termination is left to your existing proxy.

You can also set this in .env before running setup:

SKIP_REVERSE_PROXY=true

Your reverse proxy should forward traffic to localhost:5678 (n8n) with WebSocket support enabled.

⚠️ Security note: Without a domain, n8n runs over plain HTTP with no TLS and no rate limiting. This is fine for local installs (home server, LAN, testing). For a public VPS, always use a domain with HTTPS — otherwise credentials are transmitted unencrypted and the instance is exposed to the internet.

Updating

Normal update — pulls code + Docker images, restarts services. Your personality, credentials, and data are preserved:

cd n8n-claw && ./setup.sh

Full reconfigure — re-runs the setup wizard (personality, language, timezone, proactive/reactive, embedding key). Your existing data and credentials are kept, but you can change all settings:

./setup.sh --force

Use --force when you want to change your agent's name, language, communication style, or switch between proactive/reactive mode.

Troubleshooting

Agent not responding to Telegram messages? → Check all workflows are activated in n8n UI

"Credential does not exist" error? → Add the Postgres credential manually (see Step 2)

MCP Builder fails? → Make sure the LLM node in MCP Builder has Anthropic API selected

Agent shows wrong time? → Re-run ./setup.sh --force and set the correct timezone, or update it directly in user_profiles table via Supabase Studio

Heartbeat not sending messages? → Check that heartbeat_config has enabled = true for heartbeat (proactive) or morning_briefing. You can enable it via chat: "Enable the heartbeat"

Memory search returns nothing / vectorized: false? → Check your embedding API key in the tools_config table (tool_name: embedding). Without a valid key, memory still works but falls back to keyword search.

DB empty / Load Soul returns nothing? → Re-run seed: ./setup.sh (skips already-set config)

Logs:

docker logs n8n-claw        # n8n
docker logs n8n-claw-db     # PostgreSQL
docker logs n8n-claw-rest   # PostgREST

Optional: WorkflowBuilder with Claude Code

The WorkflowBuilder tool lets your agent build complex n8n workflows using Claude Code CLI. This requires additional setup:

1. Install the community node

In n8n UI → Settings → Community Nodes → Install:

n8n-nodes-claude-code-cli

2. Install Claude Code on your VPS

# Install Node.js if needed
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
apt-get install -y nodejs

# Install Claude Code CLI globally
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# Verify
claude --version

3. Configure in n8n

  • Open the WorkflowBuilder workflow
  • The Claude Code node needs access to the CLI
  • Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable in your n8n container:
# Add to docker-compose.yml under n8n environment:
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here

Then restart: docker compose up -d n8n

Without this setup, the WorkflowBuilder tool won't function — but all other agent capabilities work fine without it.

Stack

  • n8n — workflow automation engine
  • PostgreSQL — database
  • PostgREST — auto-generated REST API
  • Supabase Studio — database admin UI
  • Kong — API gateway
  • Claude (Anthropic) — LLM powering the agent
  • Telegram — messaging interface
  • SearXNG — self-hosted meta search engine (no API key needed)
  • Open-Meteo — free weather API (example MCP, no key needed)

License

MIT
